reviews.review_title: A great VR option for chess players
reviews.review_description:
This seems like a great option for people of all ages to enjoy Chess in a VR setting. A Harry Potter themed option for fans, but also a cozy room option for others who just enjoy the game of Chess but don’t have the ability to play “over the board” IRL. 
The devs are awesome. There was an issue that i did not understand and they were immediately available to help me out. 
It is feature rich and further development is in process.

Great job Devs!

developer_response.body: Thank you Jake! Feel free to ping us whenever you need. Looking forward to playing together too!

reviews.review_description: If you love chess or want to learn this is the game for you, makes it so much fun and you are transported. Easy to follow great looking and a perfect escape to play chess in this magical game. The Devs are doing a great job and although this is a work in progress it's based game is amazing. Thankyou Devs keep up the good work and I'm looking forward to the future developments 🖖

reviews.review_title: By far my favorite way to chess
reviews.review_description: This game is so good. I love the voice commands and it makes you feel straight out of Harry Potter. Hear me out you can name the pieces so that way you can go “hey Jerry! Move a space.” It just sounds so funny to me. And the design on the pawns… I want to smoosh their cute goblin faces so much. You should definitely add cosmetics. Like different sets that you can mix and match to make your own set. (Opponents could see them but could add a setting to turn that off so it doesn’t get confused.) I think if you add that the game would be the best ever. Also if you do maybe add a supporter pack. It’s like 5 bucks and gives you an exclusive unicorn style for your knight. It would only be available in early access. Any way hope you like my ideas and keep up the good work devs. I love this game and I hope you never stop improving. (The unicorn would be so great tho. Just saying.) :)

developer_response.body: Hahaha, we loved these ideas! Join our Discord and let’s dive into the details together. It's great to hear that you deeply enjoyed Merlin's Chess, and thanks for this awesome review, you truly made our day! Finally, don’t worry, we’re here to stay ^^

reviews.review_description:
Love the game so far  and looking forward to any new chess pieces coming out if so please let me know asap haha

In response to your reply I cannot work out how to add you on discord thats semi new territory for me but I’d love to get to see first haha that would be great

developer_response.body: Thanks! And yes, they're definitely coming out, but I hope you don't expect some boring stuff from us ^^ Make sure to join our Discord and ping us, so we can possibly invite you to a private version when it's ready :)

reviews.review_title: The BEST Chess app for playing online!
reviews.review_description: This app is AMAZING!! The experience is so immersive. The Chess dot com integration is a game changer for anyone who plays online. No more staring at a 2D screen. I set my first chess dot com game in a classical cozy environment listening to soft music next to a fireplace and the experience was Awesome! I know this game is still an early development app but it is a blockbuster if you love to play chess. Again the chess dot com integration is key and I assume they will also interface with Lichess in the future.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much Lhotta!
We’re incredibly happy to hear that you enjoyed the game and you clearly see what we're trying to achieve.

Merlin’s Chess is still evolving, and we’re working hard every week to make it better, deeper, and more magical. Your support and feedback help us more than you can imagine. 

About Lichess: ChessCom is our main platform partner and the biggest chess platform right now, but we'll definitely consider your feedback to offer alternatives!

reviews.review_description:
The devs asked me to leave a review, I understand it’s an early release and bugs are being quickly ironed out so I have been forgiving on that front.

Pros:
Gorgeous graphics
Customisation of game type
Unique style and atmosphere
Great animations
AI bots with 10 levels of difficulty
Link to chess dot com account opening additional versatility.
Friendly team of devs who are quick to render assistance via discord

Cons:
Animations could be faster (or customisable speed would be good), great aesthetically but slow the momentum a little.

Avatar model is androgynous (no facial hair/male or female customisations, body type A,B,C etc) probably well meaning or rooted in the devs ideological position, but it just slightly degrades the experience for me.

Overall a worthwhile purchase and I expect the devs to keep improving the game so if you enjoy chess and want novel environments to play it in I recommend this.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your honest and thoughtful review! We’re working hard to improve the game with each update, and we’ll definitely take your feedback into account for the next release.

Quick tip: while we’ll continue working on animation speed, you can already switch the animation type from Full Animation to Slide-only or Instant modes if you prefer faster gameplay, so you don’t have to sacrifice momentum 🙂

Regarding avatars, we know the game needs more customization. So a major avatar update is already part of our roadmap, and it will introduce a completely brand-new Avatar System with lots of unique elements and customization options.

Stay tuned!

reviews.review_description: Video looked great so bought the game but so disappointed. It doesn't seem finished it's rickety moving around just to get to the controls is so bad id say it's almost unusable. Says it's a work in progress but in all truth it shouldn't of been released in this state I feel ripped off. The developers need to get working on it because as it is it's junk sadly.

developer_response.body:
Hey uk.ste,
Thanks for giving Merlin’s Chess a try, and we're really sorry to hear it didn’t meet your expectations. It’s clearly an Early Access game with lots of improvements and new features on the way, but we also have thousands of players enjoying it regularly, so we'd really love to understand what didn’t land for you.

If you’re open to it, we’d be happy to invite you to chat directly with the dev team on Discord. Hearing your specific feedback helps us prioritize the right things, and in some cases the issues may already be fixed in our internal builds.

You can join us here: merlinschess.gg/discord

Thanks again for taking the time to share your thoughts.

reviews.review_description: I wish there were more users on line. Really enjoyed the interface. I spectated too which was also fun cause the players can talk to everyone. Wish I could sign in to chess dot com easier.

I love how this game has a fantasy type theme to play chess and what im most mind blown about is that chess dot com is collabed with it so you can match up with people in there too, i haven't played on that site in like 10-13 years i'm suprised people still use it! I'm glad the producers of this game is giving it their all and I give then my full support so I can try to learn to play chess again in a more fancy way!

Merlin’s Chess brings a fun take to the game of chess.  I enjoyed playing with the AI opponents, which vary in difficulty to suit your experience level, and being able to link my Chess dot com profile to play online was a bonus.  The board highlights the available moves for the pieces when you pick them up, and they come to life when you set them down.  They will move to where you placed them on the board and be ready for awesome battle animations if there is another piece occupying that square.  Merlin’s Chess is very reasonably priced, and though it’s in early access, it will only get better as the developers are actively working to improve it.

reviews.review_title: Great chess game for all audiences
reviews.review_description: From exciting visuals to a great use of audio this game puts you into a world of chess and magic. Even if you are a novice chess player like myself you could still enjoy this experience because of the decent bots that can help you learn the basics of chess and the connection with the environment which makes you immersed in the experience and in my case more likely to attempt learning chess. The reason I have rated this four stars is because I believe the visuals could go a bit further, the current ones are exciting but a bit rough around the edges overall and I believe they could be more animated when the standard pieces attack to make things a bit more energetic paired with the already existing sounds that make everything sound real and giant to further immerse the player. Otherwise, I would suggest this to anyone looking for a chess game in VR because the foundation is solid but I would also suggest this to players looking for a relaxing experience that supports hand tracking or maybe someone who is just interested in the idea of playing chess but just isn't quite sure.

It's honestly not too worth the price yet. At least it's a one time price.
But it is so buggy! I have lost matches because I have to leave and can no longer move my pieces.  Chess dot com takes so long to load or I have to restart the whole app to get it to load! Sometimes it takes several tries. Just use other free apps until it gets better. I'll try now and then and update my review if it improves.

developer_response.body:
Hey OmBeyondMaya,
Thanks for sharing your feedback. We've checked your game logs on our server, identified some of the problems you've experienced, and fixed them with our latest update. Please give it another try and let us know what you think! If you enjoy the new version, hope we can earn an updated review :) Also, please feel free to join our Discord and ping us, we'd love to invite you to a private chat with the devs! (merlinschess.gg/discord)

reviews.review_description:
This VR chess title for the Meta Quest headset is a great game. It's the best chess game in virtual reality, and it checks all the boxes for what a basic VR chess game needs. It adds some visuals and features that really make the most of the immersion that VR offers, putting you right into the game. The defining feature of this title is the ability to be on the chessboard in a first-person perspective, watching the animations of the individual chess pieces take place. This is what makes it different from any other chess game and is the main reason to get this game.
The sound is adequate—it gets the job done. The graphics, however, could have been a bit better and were probably the most underwhelming part of the game. Still, this is a minor complaint, as the game offers a lot of other options, including various pieces and environments to play in. It also has a 2D view of the chessboard that comes in handy when you are in first-person view.
You're playing chess, so the teleportation movement is fine, and it gives you a great look at the board from multiple angles. The freedom of motion is there, and overall, the game does a great job of bringing chess to VR.
If you love chess and have a Quest headset, I would highly recommend this game. While I feel like there are a few things they could have fleshed out more, there’s no reason not to buy this game if you're a fan of the genre.

Merlin's Chess is a very competent vr chess game. It features crossplay with a chess website too, so you can play with virtually anybody. I encountered very few glitches, and the visuals were very engaging. This game is made by a small team, but it stands with some of the great computer chess games from my childhood.

A few things could be improved in my opinion. The lack of adjustable options (especially mid match) is really frustrating. In particular, being forced to use teleport locomotion in 2025 is a hard ask. The lack of a mixed reality mode is also really noticeable. 

Despite my complaints, I've had a lot of fun with this. Lately, I've even been choosing it over demeo when I want that quick board game fix. I really love having vr board games with no set up or clean up process. It really feels like the future.

Great many thinks going on here and improved
ok we have AE for the moment
but cant wait to see if there in the future ever will be a option to learn to chess with some help of a bot next to us he will help and say He i should go for ... ok cheating with other people or not ? not if i and my friend want to level up or chess play and choose both to play with the help of a bot/AI seeing some move doing and pointing out why and seeing future steps ?
tricky a little team but keep going fine done

These is my Seconde Review.After a Update now you csn play vs human players with the direkt connection with chesscom. NOw you find every time an oponent. So i can recoment these App you can Player vs human vs Airlines and the atmosphäre and Environment Arena very buiteful.

reviews.review_title: WOW. Must have if you like VR chess
reviews.review_description:
I’m obsessed with chess. And VR. And this game does both perfectly. And I love Harry Potter. I think this game was made for me lol. I will continue playing this regularly. 

The visuals are stunning and the gameplay is flawless. Everything is done well and very intuitive. I love how easy it is to jump into a game. Blows my mind that a tiny dev team made this. 

I love that I can use chess .com to play on through this game, what a game changer. Just having a board to play on in rated games is invaluable.

developer_response.body:
WOW, thank you for the amazing review! 🙌
You truly made our day with your words.

It’s wonderful to meet people like you who see what we’re trying to achieve rather than just another chess game. We’re so glad it feels like the game was made for you, and honestly we feel the same way every time we play.

Feel free to ping us on Discord whenever you need support!

More is on the way. See you on the board!

reviews.review_description: hey, game is good but when will there be a patch that makes multiplayer in VR available with friends?

developer_response.body:
Hey Karasz8!
We just released our new update that introduces many ways to play together; including room codes, in-game challenge & invite, invite via Oculus UI, joining a friend via Oculus/Horizon Destinations, etc. Please give it a try and let us know what you think! If you enjoy the new update, we'd love to earn an updated review from you :) Feel free to join our Discord and ping us if you need any support!  (merlinschess.gg/discord)

reviews.review_description: Looks pretty awesome looking down on the Giant chess pieces if you enjoy chess this is definitely for you

reviews.review_title: Merlin is Back in the Game - VR Style
reviews.review_description:
Merlin’s Chess simply makes the game of chess fun and exciting again. I found Merlin’s Chess extremely intuitive, easy to navigate via its menuing layout, and super fun to play in its immersive VR-based 3D environment.

The game leaves little guesswork as to how to setup and customize its unique chess battlefield. I had the choice to control my chess pieces using my controllers, voice commands, or a combination of the two. Players can compete against an AI opponent or an actual human. The AI option has 6 levels (1-6 - level 1 is super AI forgiving) of “difficulty” to choose from, while human opponents are as challenging as their chess prowess offers. I was able to play with random chess players all over the world, with friends only, and in either ranked or unranked (casual) matches.  

Unique and in-depth game environmental customization options are one of the main components that really caught my attention to this specific game versus other available games. You can choose timers for game-play duration, each players turn, and a time setting to “undo” your move if you make a bad choice. I found all of these settings very easy to customize. Players can choose the standard chess game playfield or you can choose to watch the pieces battle one another each turn. You can also choose to show the previous move of your opponent. I like the ability to choose the backdrop “arena” setting I played in and the background music on/off choice.

In short, I really like this game. I hadn’t played chess in a while and felt a little nervous playing against either AI or another live person. I found both to be very forgiving.

I went into this game not knowing how to even play chess. I learned how to play chess from this game. It is very nice that it shows you the spots you can move to. That helped me learn a lot easier. I 10/10 recommend. Even if you don't know how to play chess, it is worth it to get the game. The visuals are super cool. I never thought I'd see my pieces destroy the enemies with a staff. Overall, this game is awesome.

Note: The game was provided for free to perform playtesting.
Some may come for the graphics and animations, but more would come for a simple but working integration to chess com and play rated games with that account. So far it is not working.

developer_response.body: Hey umega, we’re sorry to hear you’ve encountered issues. That definitely shouldn’t happen. We have hundreds of active players using our Chess.com integration without any major issues, so whatever you’re experiencing, we’d love your help to identify and resolve it as quickly as possible. Please join our Discord at merlinschess.gg/discord and ping us for instant support. We’d be happy to work with you to get it fixed.

reviews.review_description: Such a blast to play chess in this game.  The animated fantasy pieces that come to life to snuff out their foes is brilliant.  Right out of a Harry Potter or Alice in Wonderland chess experience.  I mean who hasn't read those books and want to play that version of chess compared to the stodgy real world kind?  I played chess as a kid now and then so know the basics but was never good at it.  Now I am far from a kid and thoroughly enjoyed watching the animated pieces in the arena come to life after I or my opponent made our moves on the small standard board.  Each piece has wildly different attack animations to take down the opponents piece.  But this game isn't just for novices to learn chess, or kids(or kids at heart) to revel in the animations, it is also integrated with chessdotcom for more serious or committed chess players.  I think this game is definitely built for appeal to a wide range of folks.

This game is amazing, you can play it by moving the different chess part one by one or you can make the parts move by voice !!!

You can play it with one hand or by voice, accessibility is really good !

There is a small music playing while you play, the ennemies are talking, this is funny 🤣

reviews.review_title: (I don't know how to play chess)
reviews.review_description: Firstly, I was expecting this game to be a super cheesy rendtion of chess with a relatively flat view.  But no!  The first game I jump into I tackled an AI opponent (because I'm not a seasoned cheese player), and there was a super cool animated version playing out like a movie in front of me while myself and my oponent manipulate our pieces.  This was so much fun, the physical and audible effects were completely unexpected. There are various environments, and an option to view others playing.  One view I enjoyed a lot was the stadium view of the animated pieces.  Oh, and for those who prefer the stripped down version, you can choose a plain environment with regular pieces.  An element I really enjoyed was seeing the log of moves made during the game.  There are also settings to edit your characters apperance to your liking.  This game requies minimal physical space - meaning you can sit, stand, whatever.

reviews.review_title: Turned me into a chess addict
reviews.review_description:
When I first started testing this game, I only knew the moves each piece makes, but had no real grasp of strategy. Seeing the pieces in vr really helped me see the movement lanes like I hadn't before. The experience for a beginner was outstanding. 
The interface shows you what moves are valid, and this helps you learn how to play almost instantly. 
Losing to the AI (because you will) feels more like a fun lesson and not a punishment and taught me a new strategy or showed me a weakness in my current strategies each time.
I did want to smack that goblin after losing. Being able to throw things at him would be cathartic lol.
My only suggestion would be that I would like some more lines/taunts for him, as the dialogue can be a bit repetitive.
 This game has got me thinking about chess non-stop now. I highly recommend you give it a shot.

reviews.review_title: Works well but not much to it
reviews.review_description:
Was hoping some more character skins and environments or anything else to make more of a game not just chess with some animation.

Nicest chess game in VR but was hoping for more content. 

I’m happy with other ones I have. Don’t see a reason to own another chess game

developer_response.body:
First, thank you so much for your honest review, and calling us “the nicest chess game in VR” 🙌🏽 

Let us give you a reason to make Merlin’s Chess your primary VR chess app:
It’s the ONLY chess game officially supported by Chess.com, so you can instantly start playing with 200 million Chess.com players in a 24/7 online pool, and also play against hundreds of official bots of celebrities and famous chess players.

We will definitely bring new piece sets, environments, AI opponent characters, learning features, and more. We are still in Early Access and working hard to deliver these as soon as possible, as a super small team.

We would love to work with you to make the game better. Feel free to join our Discord to share feedback, get instant support, and submit feature requests! (merlinschess.gg/discord)

Hope we can change your mind and earn an updated review :)

reviews.review_title: An immersive way to play chess
reviews.review_description:
I dont think i have to write much about the gameplay as its... Chess. Like it or hate it, you already know what the game is. Now to what makes this special, the  immersion. Theres something (literally) magical about sitting in a wizards hut or arena playing a version of chess with living pieces like you would see in some fantasy settings with their own board games. While the animations could be a bit faster, theres no pressure in the normal mode without a timer. Whats also nice is being able to join other games as spectator and just be there able to talk and be seen by the players, tho it would be nice if that i a setting. As of now in early access, it doesn't seem to be possible to turn it off.

What could almost be missed if it wasnt for the explicitly stated support is the integration with chess(dot)com, which is buried as an option in the settings menu instead of in the game select. While it is currently underdeveloped with just a single mode being available in early access, its really promising to have a way of enjoying this game without relying on a small VR playerbase or AI, so until chess(dot)com itself shuts down, this game also wont die.

Lastly, there are voice commands. It wasnt made clear on what commands work so i was on my own to try it out, but i quickly figured out how to move pieces which can make it a pretty comfortable experience if i just want to lay back and relax. Its not even that strict, i can either say "a2a4" or "a2 to a4" to make a move, it works with multiple languages and even if i say something like "knight to d7", the game will figure out which knight to take based on which one can reach d7. It feels pretty intuitive and adds to the magical feel of the game

reviews.review_title: A really great and different way to play
reviews.review_description:
The fantasy-themed animations are a great twist to chess. It feels a bit like Wizard’s Chess from Harry Potter, and watching the pieces battle adds a fun layer to the game. The VR environments are beautiful, and the sound design adds to the immersion. I also appreciate how beginner-friendly it is, with helpful move hints, tutorials, and a very forgiving AI on lower levels. Highlighted legal moves make learning easy, if you don't already know how to play. Online play and Chess(dot)com integration are a big plus, especially for tracking your rank and playing with friends. The game has support for hand tracking (which I found works great and feels more immersive than controllers), voice commands, and controller input.

That said, there are still areas that need improvement. The worst thing is that the game seems to need internet connection at all times, even when playing alone with an AI. The pacing feels a bit slow sometimes, especially when waiting for animations or AI moves to finish, and some of the attack animations could use more variety. The characters have a very small set of dialogue that becomes repetitive after some time. Also, while fun and stylish, the game might feel too basic for hardcore chess players focused on deep strategy and fast-paced play. 

Despite those issues, this is easily one of the most enjoyable chess experiences I’ve had in VR. If you're into chess and want something more than the usual flat board, this game is worth checking out—but it still needs some polish.

reviews.review_title: The Chess Game I Always Wanted
reviews.review_description: I have always had one issue or another enjoying digital chess. This one I actually enjoyed fully. I loved that I can just put my controllers down and play with hand tracking. The arenas are great, the animations are entertaining. I love the chess dot com integrations too, so I can play with my friends across the world.

If you’ve ever wanted to learn how to play chess this is a simple and fun way to get started.  The game works well, and the graphics would make chess fun for younger players.  You could get this game for a kid with no prior knowledge of game rules and they could pick it up to play immediately because picking up a chess piece automatically highlights the available moves.  Online play allows people near or far to play with people they know.

Chess .com affiliation means that players that are ready to move on to tougher opponents will have an easy time transferring.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your thoughtful review! We’re really glad to hear you’re enjoying the game. Feel free to join our Discord at merlinschess.gg/discord to directly keep in touch with the devs!

reviews.review_description:
I highly recommend this chess game! It’s incredibly easy to play, yet packed with exciting features. The voice command option is innovative, though it takes a little practice to master. Plus, the Chess dot com integration is a fantastic addition for serious players.

My favorite part? The battle animations when you capture a piece—it feels just like the giant chess scene from Harry Potter and the Philosopher’s Stone, with pieces clashing dramatically!

Seriously, give it a try. You won’t regret it!

It’s like the original Battle Chess from 1988 in a way, but now we see it in real 3D. It’s like that time we saw the pieces moving in that one movie way back in the day or the one where Harry is a wizard.  The only downside is that the pieces are only animated when they are attacking an opponent's piece. It would be nice if the pieces were more animated like that of the original Battle Chess, but this is still a nice touch. The game is a little slow as you do have to wait on animations to start/stop and return to their original movement position. There is an adjustable timer option for cancellation of moves, as the default time really makes the game drag on. You can play AI or other players or even just spectate other players, though there aren’t many social features. You can link your chess(dot)com account and continue building your record as well. The game is a nice touch on chess and could be expanded more through the future for sure.

If you love the game of chess as much as I do, I highly recommend this game! 
If you have never played the game of Chess but have always wanted to learn, the tutorial is very well done as well as during gameplay you are shown which spaces the piece that you choose can move to. 
The visual effects are engaging, and the sound of the pieces moving across the board is very realistic. There are choices of environments and types of piece sets, and I'm hopeful they will be adding more in the future. They will also be adding a story line as well. 
Did I mention this game is officially supported by Chess dot com? You can sign up and start building up your rank!
There's a lot of different options including: the skill level of the AI; choose your piece color (black or white); left or right handed; you can also edit your character and choose to play or spectate. 
My favorite thing about this game is being able to kick back, use hand tracking, and voice activation so you can speak out loud where you want your pieces positioned. It's very relaxing and challenging at the same time. 
I really appreciate the thought that went into this game, and after spending more than a few hours playing on it, I can definitely recommend this title.

Merlin's Chess is such a cool way to play in VR! I like how the pieces actually come to life with battle animations. The chess(dot)com integration is great; playing against that huge community right in VR is amazing. Seems like some nice updates are coming, like a puzzle mode, which sounds great even for casual players that aren't huge chess buffs.
Enjoyed it and even the beginner ai can cause a draw

developer_response.body: Haha, yes our AI opponent MechaGoblin is dealing with a bit of narcissism and ego issues, so his beginner levels might feel slightly more competitive than the beginner Chess.com bots 🙂

reviews.review_title: Merlin’s Beard! A VR Chess Game!
reviews.review_description:
Full disclosure: I was provided a copy of this game for free to playtest and review.

Bottom Line: with approximately 10 Chess VR games out on the Quest 3 as of this review, it can be difficult to stand out from the crowd. Merlin‘s Chess succeeds in some areas by having solo opponents and scaled difficulty, as well as classic and animated modes for those waiting a little flair while they play chess. There is also a multiplayer mode. The hand tracking works well enough as well as do the voice commands, there are multiple ways to play a game, which is appreciated.

I give this game a 4-star rating as it is still the best chess game currently on the Quest 3, and with any luck, the addition of a puzzle mode will bring new players into the fold.

This game is very different to any other chess games you might have played, but in a good way!

The active animations are very engaging, with the funny AI voice lines, it makes a perfect combo.

Highly recommend this game to anyone who enjoys chess, or has time to kill!

reviews.review_title: Needs some work but is really fun
reviews.review_description: I personally love chess, i didn’t really need this extra immersion for me to enjoy chess i thought. But this makes it way more fun, however currently the chess com integration is limited and doesn’t let you play with friends or play variants which is a shame since it would be perfect with it.

Wizards Chess or Battle chess whatever you want to call it. It’s very similar to what I would play on my old computer growing up back in 1998. You play chess and the pieces smash against each other. I think it's exactly what you’d expect from this sort of game. Chess with some fun graphics, challenging ai, and ability to play multiplayer. If you like chess and VR give it a shot.

I mean everything is amazing. I had a great time playing it and enjoyed every moment of it. The animations when taking pieces are really cool, the environments have a lot of detail and are interesting, and having online play with chess. com is great. The hand gestures are a little bit buggy and are sometimes hard to do but it’s really not that big of a problem. From my experience moving pieces is really easy and even with voice commands you can say 100 different ways to move and it’ll work. Overall it’s a great game maybe a few optimization things for fps and hand gestures but still it’s worth the money.

reviews.review_title: A twist on the original recipe.
reviews.review_description:
Looking for a way to fill in time, enjoy chess? Then look no further. 

This is everything chess is and more, gripping you with the original concept and throwing a sense of urgency and emotion. From the get go it’s an easy one to pick up and play if you grasp the basic concept of chess. Placing strategic moves in order to eliminate your opponent’s pieces has never felt more real and satisfying with animations to signify your enemy’s pieces demise. 

Visually this is nice and clear and paired with great sound effects and a foreboding music score. There’s not much else to say but if you enjoy strategy and mastering the art of chess then this may be for you.

The game has stunning visuals along with the 
good hand tracking. The sounds and visuals when moving around the game/the chess board are amazing and fun to look at(eye candy).  The game has good graphics in the menus and the functions with the table thingy when getting into a game. The game is a little hard at first trying to learn to move around and function. There also was some difficulty with figuring out how to load into a game with a ai or real player. I also had trouble with the all the graphics making my eyes hurt after a couple rounds   Even with all that the experience of being able to play aginst real people through chess. Com-makes this game a must buy

reviews.review_title: A fun new way to play chess
reviews.review_description:
Disclaimer: product received for free

If you love chess, you’ll love this game. If you hate chess, this game isn’t for you. It is essentially just chess (at least right now). But, it even has Chess dot com integration.

As far as early access experiences go, this is holding up really well. I didn’t experience any crashes, and only a few minor performance issues. The performance stutters can make you uncomfortable from time to time, but I imagine they will be fixed.

In terms of content, there are three environments to play in, and the game mode is (like I said before) just chess. 

The void environment has you playing in a dark void. It would be great if this environment could have the option to use pass through, which I think is something this game is missing. I’d love to be able to move and scale a virtual chessboard onto my real life desk for example.

The “cozy” environment is very relaxing.

And finally, the arena, with a small chess board infront of each player, and a larger chess board between the both of you, mirroring the game you are playing, that will even do special attack animations for each chess piece.

You can also customise your avatar, and even change the style of the chess pieces between fantasy and classic. This has a lot of potential for the future and I’d love to see more here, with more piece styles, maybe even board styles as well that could be unlocked.

In terms of gameplay, like I keep saying, this is just chess. There are no additional game modes offering any kind of “arcade” style twist on chess. However, they do spice up the gameplay of classic chess with the use of voice commands.

The voice commands work surprisingly well. At first I thought it was a bit of a gimmick, but it’s really intuitive, and honestly feels pretty cool.

The last thing I’ll talk about is the Chess dot com integration. Really great to see. It’s very limited right now, and it doesn’t offer many of the bells and whistles from the main game like styled pieces or special attack animations (when playing a live crossplay match). You have access to the Chess dot com bots, and, you can play live matches. However, there is currently only one timer option for live matches, that being 15 minutes. The UI for accessing it is also quite janky, for now. But again, great to see.

If you love chess, this is a fun new way to play and I’m sure you’ll enjoy it. If you don’t love chess, there’s not much here that will change your mind.

This has great presentation.
Love the design from the lodge to the arenas and chess.

I’m hoping they add smooth movement instead of just teleport though.

You can partake in a game of chess against AI or Human. Can set timers.
Or you can spectate and watch players battle it out in the middle of the arenas.

You are standing on a platform, with your opponent opposite, each making your move on the chess board in front of you, which then plays out on the huge battle board below.
To your left there is also the traditional 2D chess board, to help you analyse your next move.

I’d like if we could also hover over the enemies pieces and it would show what they are, rather than just showing what our own pieces are. Don’t matter too much, as you will get to learn what the pieces are from using your own pieces in game.

Picking up each piece also does show where that piece can be placed, which is handy for chess novices like me.

You can even go down to the board below and get in the middle of the action.

Animations are decent when each piece attacks another piece.

Sounds are good and it also has voices.

Maybe could give us option to choose from various chess sets, each with own animations? Maybe a good, evil, undead, demons, barbarians, vikings, medieval, trolls.

I was impressed with how nicely done this game is when playing.. early access but I didn’t come across any bugs so far.

I recommend this to any chess players out there.

I’m someone who has played chess extensively over the years ever since I was a kid. Constantly challenging friends and family to a chess game to hone my skills. Because of that, I’ve spent countless hours trying different apps that allow me to play the game. Many of the chess options I’ve found have always been interesting, but never really fully utilized the full extent of technology to enhance the experience. Merlin’s chess is not like that. It finds a way to keep the game interesting and immersive for any player who has an interest in learning chess, or like me is a “seasoned vet”. There’s several atmospheric arenas, and full blown animations for the pieces if you want them to make the game all the more fun to watch as well as play. Plus, it even has Chess . com integration. (Although in its current early access form it’s not perfected yet). Overall the game has a lot to offer and a lot of potential in the future if the devs continue to develop more environments and maybe even implement different chess sets with unique animations to spice it up even further. If you’re looking for an immersive vr chess game, this one might be for you.

reviews.review_title: Beautiful fun game great customer suppor
reviews.review_description:
What a great idea, my son loves Chess and Harry Potter. 

It is early Access but it feels very polished to me. Voice commands make it even more real. 

Customer support is out of this world. 

Highly recommended for everybody who plays chess.

Merlin's Chess is a amazing game to play chess against other players and bots. The early access version includes two chess piece themes, a fantasy, and a regular theme. The animations of pieces taking each other of sick! 😎 It has integration with Chess(dot)com. With you chess(dot)com account linked, you can play against chess(dot)com bots and play with other chess(dot)com players. Just a great game.

To start the game does really well of portraying that wizard setting you get from the name Merlins Chess, when I loaded in I was already getting my hopes high for the gameplay. And the gameplay didn't disapoint, loading in against a friend on the cosy map first. This allowed me to try out the mechanics, I was really inticed by the voice commands so I tended  to use those mainly. I really liked how the pieces moved and had custom animations for when you take a piece.

However this doesn't mean there were no issues. To begin with I had constant issues with voice commands not doing what I said for example: d2d3 instead did d2d4. Moving it 2 grids forward instead of the one grid I wanted. Another issue I had was on only the arena map, there was a slight glitch with the sky, It would follow the headset wherever I moved it and at somepoint during the match it became overlayed on the maps surroundings, so instead of being a background it became merged with the foreground.

Overall these issues aren't gamebreaking and will hopefully be finetuned. So im excited to see where this game progresses to with future updates and improvements.

But great experience overall and would highly recommend trying it out for yourselfs.

Merlin's Chess is pretty immersive the environment makes you feel like you're sitting across from a real opponent in a magical setting. And a real nod to the old classic Battle Chess PC game.  The graphics are clean and elegant, the animations are smooth, and the whole experience feels polished and professional for a game that is still early release.
Whether you're a beginner or a seasoned player, you can customize the level select and game play which is great for both new learners and regular chess players.  Fully recommend this game and I look forward to challenging  a real person in multiplayer the AI is great but...

reviews.review_title: Great early access VR chess game
reviews.review_description: I've never been particularly good at chess but I do enjoy playing and even more so in a VR setting. There are a couple boards to choose from in some nice fantasy settings. In front of you there is the standard chess board setup, initially it was hard for me to pick up pieces but this has since been addressed. As you grab pieces and play the game the action also plays out on a giant chess board in front of you with some minimal action to add to the game. It's a very nice welcome touch. The game also allows you to log into chess com and track your stats which is amazing. I was able to beat the ai (on the easiest level). There is also a lot of tutorials for new players so even if you have not played chess before this is a good place to start. Hopefully when some more accessibility options and boards are added this will definitely be a 5 star game. Would recommend
I've not very good at chess but this game is really fun and very well built, it has a really cool theme, I would recommend this to anyone who likes chess and vr
There is not much i can say about this game exept that its just a way to play chess in a more intresing way and i prefer it over just playing a match on my phone

I received this game free for testing.

I went into this knowing that I'm not the biggest Chess fan. I'm a huge fan of board gaming, and I could go into length why I don't like Chess, but I'm not reviewing Chess, I'm reviewing Merlin's Chess.

If you don't like chess, this isn't going to convert you. That's not it's goal. If you like chess, you should love this. It's does pretty much what it sets out to do. If you *love* chess, well I don't actually know how you'll feel. I don't play at any kind of skill level so I can't tell you if this game is good at chess.

However, it is very nice to add a little spice to the game. The animations are fun and it doesn't take itself too seriously. Adding capture animations is always a good visual upgrade. Being able to alter your perspective and play from the chess board is pretty cool as a novelty.

A big complain is the default delay for confirmation. It *really* slows the game down. It would make much more sense to have the computer be thinking during the cancel period, which based on the additional delay after the cancel period, it's not doing. You can shorten the delay in settings, but it would work better to have a chance to cancel the delay for when you are sure of your move, and the AI should be immediately ready to take it's move if you let the cancel timeout.

Unfortunately, the game does not follow standard VR controls. The trigger button is often used as grip, and sometimes the game requires you to use both the trigger and the grip. Please follow standards. It's called the "grip" button for a reason,

I also have some gripes about the animations. It needs more variety from the attacker, and it needs some reaction animation from the defender. Normally I would give this more of a pass, but since this game is all about uplifting Chess with style and graphics, the graphics and style are particularly important. I believe this is just part of being early access, though.

developer_response.body:
Hey tpancoast,
Thanks so much for your detailed feedback!

We’ve got good news about your biggest complaint :)
You can actually adjust the move confirmation time, or even turn it off completely. Just open the menu and head to the Settings > Gameplay section. We should definitely make this more visible and accessible in-game. Thanks for flagging it!

As for animations, no worries at all! We don’t have walking animations for the chess pieces, and we don’t plan to add any for now. Instead, we’ll be introducing new piece sets with more animation and reaction variety as you've mentioned. We'll also update the animations of the AI opponent pretty soon!

We’re also working on something that might help attract more non-chess people into this world, and we’d love to have you as one of our early testers again.

Hope we can earn an updated review from you :)

reviews.review_description: I do love chess and the way they have brought it to VR is fantastic and the throwing is brilliant, the ai in this is challenging making the experience so much better. I would recommend this game to anyone who enjoys chess and vr. :)

reviews.review_title: I've been waiting for this game!
reviews.review_description:
I used to play chess in VR, but I eventually got bored with how simple and empty it felt. I’m so glad someone finally came up with something like this. With the official Chess com integration, it really feels like a breath of fresh air!

I’ve always loved Harry Potter and played almost all the games based on it. As a casual chess player, I’ve often found myself wishing I could play something that felt like that. This game totally captures the Wizard’s Chess vibe (or should I say that "barbaric chess game"?). I’m really into it!

I love the chess universe they are building. It has so much potential. I know it's in early access, but I’d love to see more piece sets, new animations, and maybe even customizable personal rooms in the future.

Thanks for making this game. This is probably going to be my new “just one more match” addiction!

This game brought back fond memories of a very old 80s DOS game called battle chess, where the game pieces attacked each other when you took the opponent's piece, before that computer chess was limited to flat symbols on a flat board it was ground breaking and made chess way more fun to play.  This game is like that, it brings to life the pieces in VR you stand (or sit) above in an arena and move your pieces on a board in front of you or you can voice command them to move, and below in the arena they spring to action following your commands and battle it out or you can opt for a more traditional mode seated across from your opponent in front of a board.  The game follows standard chess rules

reviews.review_title: the only chess_com supported game so far
reviews.review_description: other vr chess games don't have this feature, so they are almost dead because of player pool. I'm so happy that we're finally got chess game with this basic and fundamental feature. more time control option is required but it's already great.

reviews.review_title: Who knew chess could be this  fun
reviews.review_description: I really didnt think chess was fun but this game makes it fun and in vr takes it over the edge . its single user or multiplayer i like that there are wizards ,creatures and elves which makes it unique and the voice command take the game over the edge . the chess pieces are very cool because they come to life . I like that you can choose the arena .This game has great graphics and music too

For anyone who's into chest or wanna learn, this is it for you. I've only dabble in chest when I was little so I'm pretty much a complete noob at it but I was able to learn some just by playing this game, and that just crazy. It's friendly for anyone who's got motion sickness, visual and audio are quite engaging. Would recommend for anyone who's interested in chess.

This is a fresh take on an old game.  Chess has been around a long time and this game adds a little action to the mix.  
The game can be played with controllers or hand tracking and both work well.  It’s also nice to have voice control.  Though in timed games can be a little slow.  The pieces can be a normal board or use a fantasy theme.  I like the fantasy theme though it did take a little getting used too. 
  There are three different environments and boards to choose from. Each of them has a great design and immersion.  While 2 of them have larger boards to mimic your moves played out in giant pieces.  The actions used when taking a piece adds a little excitement to the game however the checkmate pop up is a little bland.  
  There is chess. C o m integration which makes finding matches easy or if you just want to practice your skills they have ai bots you can play.  The bots difficulty ranges from 1-10.

I do love this game as so many diffrent ideas has been put into THIS Chess  game.
I have played chess for many years on and off and I am a poor player but love the game.
 I love the letters and numbers along the board so you and speak the moves you want make as well as it being a tool helping you to lean the positions of the board.
I love the fantasy figures that fight 
( it would be lovley to see other intresting Animated sets?) And you
Can play the classic pieces to.
I love the Arena where you can play the big board and also have spectators watching  or play online players to play against. 
I found the voice repetitive to be honest after a while but you can turn this down.
I also like the background music I have a few chess sets on the quest 3 and feel this could be the best yet!
One thing I found upsetting is if your winning a game with loads of pieces and your trying for a checkmate the games finishes  with a stalemate  notice ( a draw) which is a real anti climax.
As if i am winning I do like to continue play untill I can position myself to make a checkmate ( maybe a sandbox?) but other than that point I think its my favourite quest 3 chess game.
And it's early access so still many possibilities, it gets five stars from me.
Love it !!

Merlin's Chess is a neat little game with a lot of potential. It’s still in early access so it feels a bit bare bones at times. But the gameplay itself is fun, and I really like how you can switch between different views during a match. There is even an option to set a cancel timer after each move, perfect for someone learning how to play.
I’m hopeful we’ll see more added down the line like new pieces, different environments, stuff like that. Overall, it’s a solid start and I’m excited to see where it goes.

I've played some chess games both in and out of VR and this has to be my favorite by far, I love the fact that you can use both controller or hand tracking and even have voice commands which are really great when your playing seated and your playspace might be limited, the AI is really good, in fact it might be a little too good as the level 1 AI feels like a level 3 or level 5 in terms of most chess game's AI but other than the AI difficulty I have no issues, I have yet to play against another player in this game but the game is great.

reviews.review_title: It's Chess! But how you would imagine it
reviews.review_description: It's chess with a great overworld and all the features you wish a VR-Chess game would have. Gigantic 3D-Statues that move around when you move pieces, it has multiple difficutlies and even a PvP mode to play against other people or even spectate people playing. Other than that it's chess, if you like chess and have VR, give it a go!

I have not played chess in years. This game has made me want to play more. The game comes to life as you are playing. If you first make an account on Chess dot com all your stats playing on Merlin's Chess will be linked in the stats here. 

It has tutorials to learn how to play the game. That means the game is great for even a new chess player to learn and have fun. You have two boards to choose from. You can use your hands to pick up your chess piece to move or you can use your voice to move your pieces. The board in front of you is to move your chess piece on, but then the big board above your board is animated where the game pieces move and attack in action. There are multiple difficulties to choose, time can be adjusted, boards to pick and there are more options to adjust how you play the game. You can play against AI or other players

This game has great quality even in the early access release. Looks like there are going to be more accessibility options and boards added through updates. The Developers listen to what is wrong or that can be added to make the game better.

reviews.review_title: Other chess apps feels flat after this
reviews.review_description: The first time I moved a piece with my voice and it came to life, I couldn't stop grinning. It's classic strategy at heart, but dropped into a fantasy world full of animated battles, enchanted arenas, and that "wait… am I in a movie?" kind of vibe. If you've ever watched wizard's chess scenes and thought, "I want to be on that board," well, now you kind of are.

Merlin’s Chess combines the magic I’ve grown up loving with some of the top VR tech of today! The immersive environments and animated pieces are otherworldly. Standing right on the chess board as your pieces fight to the death adds a whole new dimension to chess.

This game utilizes hand tracking, voice commands, LIV camera, and so much more, making it oh-so-simple to hop in and enjoy. The Chess. com integration means I always have opponents in random lobbies, and can keep ranking up as I play in VR. Playing against my friends is fun and accessible, and when they’re not around, the AI easiest difficulty is perfect for me, LOL.

I definitely recommend Merlin’s Chess to anyone, especially fantasy lovers and VR enthusiasts!

Merlin's Chess is a very high quality early access release. The AI is fantastic, and the ability to link your chess account is awesome.

The ambience and music are nice, and the fantasy pieces are cool. You can also use more standard pieces. It is fun to be able to go to the big board and look among the pieces as they move.

There is a good selection of settings from locomotion to turn timers, and these options are quite fully featured already.

A few minor quibbles are the loading times, and the primary board on which you move pieces gets blurry and aliases when turning your head.

reviews.review_title: They brought Wizard's Chess to life!
reviews.review_description: I had a lot of fun playing this. I'm glad that I can change the AI difficulty because it allows me to practice and learn as I go. Also, the fact that you can play with Chess(dot)com players is crazy! Gonna have to try to play with friends.

An interesting presentation of the classic game. The resolution could be a bit higher here and there (particularly noticeable with the small chessboard in front of you), and some of the effects on the large board could use a bit of fine-tuning (is the game already optimized for Quest 3?), but overall, it looks pretty good and plays well. The field of view seemed a bit cluttered—at least in the world I chose—because you're sitting in front of your small chessboard, with the large chessboard behind it and another 2D board to the side. Perhaps it would be good if you could adjust how many chess squares you see. Unlockable piece sets with new effects would also be a nice addition. And free color selection for the pieces would also be good; the board was a bit dark at times, and the black pieces on the large board weren't that easy to see. I haven't tested multiplayer yet. Overall, I definitely recommend it!

This is really cool looking Chess game with real like Chess figurines. Everything Works like charm and you could redo your movement in 5 second after you place your movement. Music is great and sounds is pretty unique. Also your CPU opponent talks here and there. You could also be challenged by online opponent. 

I am not professional Chess player but I really do enjoy taking matches in this great enviroment.
Doesn't work cant even get passed starting the game

developer_response.body: Hey! We found the problem was that your username (30 characters) is longer than we were supporting (24 characters). Thanks to your feedback, we fixed this and the update is already live 🎉 You should be able to play now without any issues. Give it another try and let us know how it goes. We’d love to see you back on the board!

reviews.review_description: My friend and I like to play chess together, but sadly there aren't many br games, that support it and do it in such a creative way. I think that the Chess figures' models look awesome, a fresh take on the cold school design. Can't forget to mention the animations, that play out every time you kill a figure. I Should add, that the animation is way too long. It might be just me, but waiting for so long just to hit a figure is unnecessary. Won't lie, I didn't see the "Cancel" button any useful, and it was just time consuming, until it gave me a win. Definitely a great thing to have. Also the game does make you feel like you're in that scene from Harry Potter, I recommend.

reviews.review_title: A very aesthetically pleasing chess game
reviews.review_description: Received the game for previewing purposes, as someone who learned how to (barely) play chess like a month ago, I found this game as a pleasant and welcoming experience, something I would play for a quick round or two with a buddy, or practicing for a chess tournament. Really excited to see where this goes

This game is fun to play. I played against a.i. and human. the settings allow you to adjust the game to make it go faster by turning down the cancel move timer. the animations are cool. I don't like losing so im happy to he undefeated 🙌

reviews.review_title: Chess in VR with visual enhancement
reviews.review_description: Received for free to play test. Chess in VR definitely adds a dimension to the game and makes it more interesting/engaging. The AI opponent comments on your moves and when striking a piece there are nice animations. What I liked was that you can use voice commands to play, which makes it very accessible and also makes you feel more like a commander that is guiding your troops. Visual style is geared towards teenagers. Unfortunately the game showed me that I haven't played chess in a long time and couldn't win a match (there are multiple difficulties to choose from), but that is good motivation

I love the environments for being so cozy and fantasy styled, it goes amazingly with the fantasy chess pieces. Playing chess of course is playing chess, and the game does what it is expected to do. Being able to connect it to your chess account is a neat little feature here.
The devs took care of issues within the game quickly thus made the experience a lot nicer.
However I would like to see more variety in character designs and avatar customisations.
Especially in the online multiplayer customisation is a great thing (opposed to everyone looking the same).
Overall, it's a neat chess experience, with some handy features to help you out (log, screen, button in case of misplacing). However still in a need for a little polishing here and there, mainly graphics

reviews.review_title: Real chess with great hand tracking!
reviews.review_description: I have never been a fan of hand tracking before. I have always found it to be more frustrating and less immersive than using controllers, but the hand tracking works sooo well in this game that I actually prefer it! It makes it feel like real chess. I also love how customizable your gameplay is. You can turn up the AI intelligence for a real challenge or if your chess skills are subpar at best like me, turn it down. Great animations and an awesome game if you love chess or are like me, who very casually plays.

With VR and voice commands you can fully replicate what it's like to command an army of enchanted stone in a battle of wits. 
The kill animations are quite satisfying. There are a couple different environments to try out. I recommend going down to the chess board to truly feel the scale of the game. The ai has 10 levels of difficulty. You can also link your chess . com account if you want. 
If you are looking for a vr way to play chess then this is a fantastic way to do so.
